metrics.narrative: No quantified business problem, cost savings target, headcount impact, cycle-time reduction, or ROI case has been documented in any Salesforce field or buyer conversation. The opportunity was created 30 Apr 2026 but no discovery call, needs-analysis task, or business-objectives field has been populated. Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c, and Compelling_Event__c are all empty. With zero buyer engagement in 57 days, no pain or value case has been articulated. The immediate move is to conduct a discovery call with Shaun Adkins to quantify the procurement pain (manual PO workflows, maverick spend, supplier fragmentation, approval cycle delays) and establish a baseline ROI model anchored to Lakeside's spend under management and current process cost.

sources: OpportunityContactRole: Shaun Adkins flagged Decision Maker but no champion role assigned
Salesforce: X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c, Champion_Identified__c both null
Next_Step references his demo interest but no logged follow-up or engagement since 05 Jun (21 days ago)
No Avoma calls, no Tasks showing advocacy or internal selling behavior
champion.narrative: No champion has been developed. The sole contact role is Shaun Adkins (Decision Maker, Director Strategic Sourcing), and Next_Step indicates he expressed demo interest, but there is zero evidence of advocacy, internal selling on our behalf, or active engagement. No call logs Shaun introducing other stakeholders, sharing Lakeside's internal evaluation timeline, pushing back on a competitor for us, or driving the agenda forward. With 57 days of silence since opp creation and no logged follow-up after his stated July 2026 demo interest, Shaun's engagement level is unknown and possibly cooling. The fields X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c and Champion_Identified__c are empty. The immediate move is to re-engage Shaun (call or email by 01 Jul, before his stated July availability window closes), deliver value in the first touch (share a relevant Merlin negotiation use-case win or a book-distribution peer reference), and assess his responsiveness and willingness to introduce us to the Economic Buyer or VP Operations. If he is unresponsive after two attempts over 10 days, explore alternate entry via the relevant stakeholder (VP/Controller, account contact) or another sourcing/operations leader.

sources: Salesforce: Competitors__c null; How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c, Existing_vendor__c, Replacing_What__c all null
No Avoma calls, no Next_Step or Task content referencing competitors or incumbent
No RFP scope or shortlist visibility
competition.narrative: The competitive landscape is completely unknown. The Salesforce Competitors__c field is null, and no free-text field (How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c, Existing_vendor__c, Replacing_What__c, Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c) names a current vendor, incumbent system, or peer evaluation. No call, Next_Step, or Task references another vendor (Coupa, SAP Ariba, GEP, Jaggaer, Ivalua) or a do-nothing/status-quo posture. With zero buyer engagement it is unclear whether Lakeside is running a multi-vendor RFP, replacing an incumbent, or exploring procurement automation for the first time. The wedge and differentiation strategy cannot be tailored without this. The immediate move is to ask Shaun directly in the re-engagement discovery call: 'How are you handling procurement today — manual/ERP-native, or is there an existing platform? Are you looking at other vendors alongside us?' Document his answer verbatim and use it to position Zycus's eProcurement ease-of-use and Merlin's AI negotiation intelligence as the wedge if peer SaaS vendors (Coupa, Ariba) or a do-nothing baseline emerge.

sources: Salesforce: Customer_Business_Problem__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, Compelling_Event__c all null
Next_Step implies demo interest but does not name a pain or compelling event
No Avoma calls, no Tasks documenting pain discovery
identify_pain.narrative: The specific operational or financial pain driving Lakeside's interest in eProcurement + Merlin is unknown. Next_Step references Shaun's demo interest after an event, implying awareness-stage curiosity rather than urgent pain, but no call or field articulates the underlying problem. Customer_Business_Problem__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, What_if_this_is_not_done__c, and Compelling_Event__c are empty. Common procurement pains for a book distributor include manual PO workflows creating fulfillment delays, maverick spend eroding supplier discounts, lack of real-time inventory/supplier visibility, and rising operational cost-per-transaction. Without a named pain owner (Director Sourcing, VP Operations, CFO) and quantified impact, the deal has no internal urgency. The immediate move is a discovery call with Shaun to surface the top 2-3 pain points (quote his words), quantify one (e.g. 'we process 8,000 POs/month manually, each taking 15 minutes, costing us $X in labor'), and identify who owns that pain and would sponsor a solution.

sources: Salesforce: Current_Contract_Expiration__c null; no contracting or legal fields populated
No Avoma calls, no Tasks referencing MSA, SOW, InfoSec, or integration scope
Stage Qualified — paper process typically surfaces at Shortlisted or later
paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics (MSA, SOW, Order Form), legal review, InfoSec questionnaire requirements, procurement channel (direct or via a partner/reseller), and any ERP/systems integration scoping are completely undocumented. No call, task, or Salesforce field (Current_Contract_Expiration__c is null) references an incumbent contract expiration, a renewal forcing function, or Lakeside's standard vendor onboarding process. With the deal in early Qualified stage and dark for 57 days, paper-process visibility is premature until discovery and Economic Buyer mapping are complete. Once a champion and EB are secured and the evaluation advances past demo, the move is to ask Shaun (or the EB/procurement lead) for Lakeside's standard vendor agreement template, InfoSec requirements, and IT integration touchpoints (ERP system, SSO, data-residency rules) so Zycus can pre-clear any redline blockers and tee up a fast contract close.

sources: OpportunityContactRole: Shaun Adkins (Decision Maker) — Director-level, not executive budget authority

economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer has been identified. The sole contact role is Shaun Adkins, Director Strategic Sourcing, flagged as Decision Maker but a Director-level sourcing role typically influences vendor selection rather than controlling a $100k budget. The Salesforce fields X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c, Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c, and Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c are empty, and no call or task references budget authority, CFO involvement, or C-level sponsorship. One account-level contact, Craig Ward (VP/Controller), may hold or influence budget authority given the finance title, but he has no contact role on this opportunity and no logged engagement. The next move is to ask Shaun directly who approves a $100k eProcurement + Merlin investment and secure an introduction to that executive (likely VP Finance, CFO, or CPO).

sources: Salesforce: What_is_the_decision_process__c, Mandate__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c all null
No Avoma calls logging process discussion
No Tasks documenting evaluation or approval workflow
decision_process.narrative: The buyer's decision process, approval chain, and timeline are completely unknown. The fields What_is_the_decision_process__c, Mandate__c, Multiple_approvals__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c, and Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c are all empty. No call or task describes whether Lakeside is running a formal RFP, a three-vendor bake-off, a sole-source evaluation, or an incumbent-replacement cycle. The single contact role (Shaun, Decision Maker) suggests he drives vendor selection, but the approval and contracting steps beyond that are unmapped. With no engagement in 57 days the process has not started or has stalled before launch. The next move is to re-engage Shaun, ask explicitly how Lakeside selects and approves a procurement platform (who evaluates, who scores, who signs), and map that process into a mutual close plan with named milestones and owners.

sources: Salesforce: Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c all null
No demo delivered; Next_Step references future demo interest only
No Avoma calls, no Tasks logging evaluation criteria or RFP scope
decision_criteria.narrative: No evaluation criteria, RFP requirements, use-case priorities, or technical/functional fit checklist has been documented. The fields Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c, and Gaps_identified_during_sales_demo__c are empty. No demo has been delivered (Next_Step references future July 2026 demo interest but it has not occurred), so no test-case validation, workshop feedback, or scoring rubric exists. Without criteria visibility Zycus cannot tailor the demo or position against peer vendors. The immediate move is to run a discovery call that extracts Lakeside's top 3-5 must-have capabilities (e.g. supplier catalog coverage, mobile approvals, Merlin-powered negotiation intelligence, ERP integration, spend analytics) and any RFP structure or evaluation timeline, then map those to our eProcurement + Merlin feature set before the demo.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AI fit signal is unknown. AIS fields (AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c) are null and no buyer conversation has surfaced Lakeside's AI maturity, agentic-automation interest, or Merlin-specific appetite. Products in scope include Merlin but it is unclear whether that was seller-suggested or buyer-requested. Without discovery the tier cannot be reliably assigned. Hypothesis: book distribution/publishing verticals often lag in procurement AI adoption (AI Curious at best), but a forward-leaning sourcing director may be AI Hungry if competitive margin pressure is high. The re-engagement discovery call must probe this directly.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 30 Apr 2026, 57 days ago. Stage: Qualified. Days to close: 300 (close 2027-04-22). Time in Qualified: 57 days. Last modified 05 Jun 2026 (21 days ago, likely admin/routing). No LastActivityDate on record. Next_Step references July 2026 demo interest but no logged activity since. A typical enterprise Qualified-to-close cycle is 12-15 months; 300 days is within range but the deal has been silent since inception, so no forward velocity to assess. Zero buyer engagement in 57 days forces Off Track regardless of stage or days-to-close.

north_star_verdict.headline: Deal remains dark with zero buyer engagement in 57+ days since creation. No calls, emails, or meetings logged. Single-threaded to Shaun Adkins with no champion, no EB, no pain quantified, and competitive landscape unmapped. July demo interest mentioned but never.

flags: RE-ENGAGE IMMEDIATELY with a value-led outreach. Shaun expressed demo interest for July 2026 (now imminent, 5 days out) but no follow-up has been logged in 21 days. Call or email by 01 Jul with a concrete value hook (e.g. 'Shaun, ahead of our July demo I wanted to share how a peer book distributor used Merlin to negotiate 8% better supplier terms on their top 50 SKUs — happy to walk through their playbook on our call'). Assess his responsiveness; if no reply in 10 business days, pivot to the relevant stakeholder (VP/Controller, account contact) or another sourcing/operations leader to find an alternate entry point. Without re-engagement in the next 7 days this deal will remain dormant past the stated demo window and should be disqualified.
best_practice_check.summary: This early-stage deal (Qualified, 57 days old, dark since inception) has zero MEDDPICC coverage and no buyer engagement, so win-strategy best practices focus on restarting momentum and building the foundation for a structured enterprise sale.

competitive_position.summary: Competitive landscape is completely unknown. No competitor, incumbent vendor, or peer evaluation has been documented in any Salesforce field, call, or task. It is unclear whether Lakeside is running a multi-vendor RFP, replacing an existing procurement system, or exploring automation for the first time (greenfield). Without this visibility Zycus cannot tailor the wedge or differentiation narrative. The immediate move is to ask Shaun directly how Lakeside handles procurement today and whether other vendors are being evaluated, then position eProcurement's ease-of-use and Merlin's AI-powered negotiation intelligence as the wedge once the competitive frame is clear.

Products in scope include Merlin (AI-powered negotiation and sourcing intelligence), but without discovery it is unknown whether Lakeside values AI differentiation or views it as a nice-to-have. The Next_Step does not reference Merlin or AI capabilities. The immediate move is to probe Shaun's AI interest in the re-engagement discovery call (e.g. 'Are you exploring AI to automate supplier negotiations or spend analytics?') and, if appetite exists, lead the demo with a Merlin use-case win (e.g. a peer distributor using Merlin to negotiate 8-12% better terms on top suppliers) rather than burying it as a feature checklist item.

deltas.subject: RUN DISCOVERY BEFORE THE DEMO to extract pain, quantify impact, map the Economic Buyer, and surface decision criteria. A demo delivered blind (no pain context, no EB in the room, no criteria) wastes the highest-leverage moment in the cycle. In the re-engagement call ask Shaun: (1) What procurement pain is driving this exploration (manual workflows, maverick spend, lack of supplier visibility)? Quantify one metric (PO volume, cycle time, cost per transaction). (2) Who approves a $100k investment — can you introduce us to that executive before the demo? (3) What are your top 3-5 must-have capabilities, and are you evaluating other vendors? Document his answers verbatim, then tailor the demo to his stated pain and criteria, and invite the Economic Buyer to attend or schedule a separate EB briefing immediately after.

deltas.subject: BUILD MULTI-THREAD AND CHAMPION COVERAGE in parallel with discovery. This deal is dangerously single-threaded to Shaun (Director-level Decision Maker) with no champion, no Economic Buyer, and no executive sponsorship. Even if Shaun is responsive, a single point of contact creates ghost risk (he leaves, priorities shift, budget reallocates). After the initial re-engagement, ask Shaun to introduce us to one peer (VP Operations, CPO, or the finance lead who controls budget) and one subordinate (a procurement manager or end-user who will validate ease-of-use and advocate internally). Position it as de-risking the evaluation for him ('I want to make sure the platform fits your team's day-to-day workflows and has exec-level buy-in before we invest in a full POC'). Simultaneously, pursue Craig Ward (VP/Controller, account contact) as a parallel finance/budget thread if Shaun does not volunteer an EB introduction within 14 days.

packets.subject: PLACEHOLDER CLOSE DATE. Close 2027-04-22 (300 days out) is a wide-open placeholder with no mutual close plan, no milestone map, and no buyer-confirmed timeline. Without discovery and process mapping the date is arbitrary.

value.flag: RE-ENGAGE IMMEDIATELY with a value-led outreach. Shaun expressed demo interest for July 2026 (now imminent, 5 days out) but no follow-up has been logged in 21 days. Call or email by 01 Jul with a concrete value hook (e.g. 'Shaun, ahead of our July demo I wanted to share how a peer book distributor used Merlin to negotiate 8% better supplier terms on their top 50 SKUs — happy to walk through their playbook on our call'). Assess his responsiveness; if no reply in 10 business days, pivot to the relevant stakeholder (VP/Controller, account contact) or another sourcing/operations leader to find an alternate entry point. Without re-engagement in the next 7 days this deal will remain dormant past the stated demo window and should be disqualified.
